slides.title: Remix & Express Mastery
slides.subtitle: Focused on Web Standards. Built for the Edge.

list.text: Elite Technical Architect
Full-stack & Performance Lead

box.title: Aravind Govindhasamy

list.text: Standardized: Code works everywhere the web does.
Less Framework JS: Use what the browser already knows.

box.title: Remix leverages browser primitives like Fetch, Request, Response, and URLSearchParams.

list.text: Resilient: Network failures don't break your app.
Fast: Immediate interaction with standard HTML forms.

box.title: Apps that work without JavaScript, then 'upgrade' to a rich SPA experience when JS loads.
Remix is built entirely on the Fetch API, making it runtime-agnostic (Node, Bun, Workers).

list.text: Transferable: Knowing MDN is knowing Remix.
Stable: Web standards evolve slowly and reliably.

box.title: By sticking to standards, Remix avoids 'framework lock-in' and ensures your skills are transferable.
Remix makes it easy to leverage browser and CDN caching with standard headers.

slides.title: 2. The Power of Nested Routing
slides.subtitle: How Remix handles complex layouts with ease.

list.text: Intuitive: Files map directly to URLs.
Organized: Easy to locate and manage route logic.

box.title: Routes are defined by your file structure in the `app/routes` directory.

list.text: Partial Updates: Only the changed parts of the UI re-render.
Parallel Data Fetching: Remix fetches data for all active routes in parallel.

box.title: The killer feature of Remix. Nested routes allow you to nest UI and data dependencies.
Used in parent routes to render their matching child routes.
Remix allows you to catch errors locally at the route level, preventing the whole app from crashing.

list.text: $id.tsx: Dynamic IDs in the URL.
$.tsx: Catch-all for 404s or flexible paths.

box.title: Handle dynamic segments and fallback pages easily with special naming conventions.

slides.title: 3. Conventional Data Handling
slides.subtitle: How Remix manages data fetching and mutations.

box.title: A server-side function that provides data to a route before it renders.
A hook that provides the data returned from the loader to your component on the client.
Actions handle non-GET requests (POST, PUT, DELETE) and are usually triggered by forms.

list.text: Automatic: No need to manually trigger refetching.
Efficient: Only active routes are re-validated.

box.title: Remix automatically re-runs all active loaders after an action completes, ensuring the UI is always in sync with the server.

list.text: End-to-End Safety: Catch data mismatch errors at compile time.
Shared Schema: Use Zod or similar tools for validation in loaders and actions.

box.title: Leveraging TypeScript to ensure that the data you fetch and send is strictly typed across the boundary.

slides.title: 4. Resilient User Experiences
slides.subtitle: Building apps that thrive in imperfect conditions.

box.title: Remix encourages using standard HTML forms for data mutations.

list.text: Instant Feedback: The app feels incredibly fast.
Automatic Rollback: Remix handles failures and reverts the state.

box.title: Updating the UI immediately after a user action, assuming the server request will succeed.

box.code:
const navigation = useNavigation();
const isSubmitting = navigation.state === 'submitting';
box.title: A hook that gives you access to the current navigation state (idle, loading, submitting).

list.text: Robust: Critical paths are always accessible.
Inclusive: Better accessibility for all users.

box.title: Because Remix uses standard forms and links, the core functionality of your app works even if JavaScript fails to load or is disabled.

list.text: Predictable: Users never wonder if their click 'worked'.
Polished: Seamless transitions between views.

box.title: Providing clear visual feedback for every user interaction using navigation states.

slides.title: 5. Powering the Backend
slides.subtitle: Running Remix on a custom Express server.

box.code:
import { createRequestHandler } from '@remix-run/express';
app.all('*', createRequestHandler({ build }));
box.title: Remix uses adapters to run on different runtimes. The `@remix-run/express` adapter bridges the gap between web standards and Node's `req/res`.

list.text: Authntication: Use Passport or custom session logic.
Logging: Morgan or Winston for detailed request traces.

box.title: Leverage the vast ecosystem of Express middleware for auth, logging, and performance.
Host your Remix app and a separate REST/GraphQL API on the same Express server.

list.text: Server-only: DB passwords and API keys stay hidden.
Client-safe: Injecting public keys into the window object.

box.title: Managing secrets on the server and safely exposing necessary config to the client.
Routes that return something other than HTML (JSON, PDF, CSV, Images).
Fully dynamic title, description, and OG tags based on route data.

list.text: Sharable: Share a link directly to a specific modal state.
Clean: UI logic remains separate from global state.

box.title: Creating modals that are accessible via their own URL, allowing for easy sharing and deep linking.

list.text: Orchestration: Combining data from multiple sources.
Performance: Parallelizing external requests on the server.

box.title: Mastering complex business logic within loaders and actions, including orchestration of multiple downstream APIs.

list.text: Edge-first: Lowest latency for users globally.
Node.js: Full power of the Node ecosystem.

box.title: Remix works anywhere that supports web standards. Deploy to Cloudflare Workers, Vercel, Fly.io, or your own server.
Running Remix on the edge with zero cold starts and instant global delivery.

list.text: Global: Deploy clusters of servers worldwide.
Proximity: Run your app and DB in the same region.

box.title: A perfect home for Express-based Remix apps, allowing you to run close to your users and your database.
Optimizing your Remix build for size and speed before deployment.
Streaming the HTML to the client as it's being generated, improving 'Time to First Byte' and perceived performance.

list.text: Predictive: Anticipate user intent for zero-lag navigation.
Lightweight: Only fetches what's actually needed.

box.title: Remix can prefetch data and assets for the next page as soon as a user hovers over a link.
Using the `meta` function to ensure every page is perfectly indexed by search engines.

list.text: Dynamic: Sitemaps update as your content does.
Controlled: Manage crawler access easily.

box.title: Generating critical SEO assets dynamically via resource routes.

slides.title: 9. Reliable Remix Apps
slides.subtitle: Testing strategies for the full stack.

box.title: Blazing fast unit tests for your Remix components, loaders, and actions.

list.text: Realistic: Catch bugs that unit tests miss.
Fast: Run tests in parallel across multiple browsers.

box.title: Testing the entire user journey, from clicking buttons to database mutations, in real browsers.

box.code:
// Mocking the database
vi.mock('./db', () => ({ get: vi.fn() }));
box.title: How to isolate and test your server-side logic by mocking external dependencies.

list.text: Holistic: Ensure middleware and routes align.
Contract Testing: Validate the API boundary.

box.title: Verifying that your Remix app and Express backend work together seamlessly.

list.text: Standards: Future-proof your skills.
Speed: Best-in-class performance through SSR and edge delivery.
Express: The solid foundation for every project.

box.title: A powerful combination of modern web standards and industry-standard backend reliability.

list.text: Adoption: Remix is quickly becoming the first choice for full-stack apps.
Evolution: Expect even better dev tooling and edge compatibility.

box.title: Where the ecosystem is heading and how to stay ahead of the curve.

list.text: Project: Build a real-world app with Remix and Express.
Docs: Deep dive into the official Remix documentation.

box.title: Start building! The best way to learn Remix is to ship something to production.
